sources_audited.note: The Census page supports the official client-owned-materials scope, labeling/imprinting, and examples. It does not measure eligibility, retained automation economics, or year-five operator requirement, for which the packet uses explicit estimates or bridges.

The BLS table supports the stated broader NAICS 561900 occupation mix, including packaging-machine operators, hand packers, inspectors, and material movers; it is not a six-digit 561910 staffing table.
O*NET supports the cited machine-packaging tasks, including labeling, inspection, line monitoring, jam clearing, adjustment, and machine tending.
O*NET supports the cited hand-packaging tasks, including inspection, weighing, counting, recording, sealing, labeling, moving, and loading materials.

PMMI supports the cited warehouse-automation facts, including labor pressure, palletizers and mobile robots, less than 20% mostly automated, and very few fully automated; the population is packaging-adjacent CPG warehousing.

The PMMI report page supports packaging-sector discussion of early AI use, machine vision, predictive maintenance, analytics, interoperability, training, and startup collaboration; it covers a broad executive participant base.
The MHI/Deloitte release supports the five-year AI and robotics adoption-intention claims, investment levels, survey size, respondent seniority, and broad manufacturing and supply-chain population.

Packaging World reports the cited 67% outsourcing intention, three-quarters provider expansion expectation, and buyer reasons for using contractors. It combines contract packaging and manufacturing and does not measure eligibility, retention, or realized five-year demand.
Gallup supports the cited 22% five-year sell-or-transfer intention among employer-business owners, while remaining a general intention survey rather than completed 561910 transfers.

Capstone supports the stated July 2026 broad-sector conditions: subdued volume, buyer selectivity, strategic dominance, operational-improvement focus, and preference for resilient or differentiated assets.
The BLS table supports the cited NAICS 561900 constant-dollar output values of $72.2 billion in 2024 and $91.8 billion in 2034 and the 2.4% annual growth rate.

caveats: The a estimate is not calibrated from a six-digit occupation mix or task-time study; the four-digit BLS mix and cross-industry O*NET task lists support direction but not the 0.44 base value.

The rho range translates technology intentions and packaging-adjacent adoption evidence into realized five-year implementation for smaller contract packagers; capital, integration, validation, and short-run variability remain unobserved at the target population.
S1 and S8 frame the service model but do not measure the e or q values; both primitives are appropriately labeled ESTIMATE, and the resulting MODEL_CONDITIONED action exposes this limitation.
The s5 bridge relies on general employer-owner intentions and broad print, paper, packaging, and equipment M&A rather than a completed-transfer denominator for eligible NAICS 561910 firms.

The d5 and o evidence combines four-digit output projections with North American contract packaging/manufacturing survey results, so the narrower six-digit service industry's future volume and operator-required share remain uncertain.

The packet marks the lens as non-heterogeneous even though its own caveats distinguish simple labeling and folding from regulated blister packaging and complex kitting; this does not change the registered ranges or base action but should temper use of the industry-wide point estimates.
summary: Mechanics pass and all 11 primitive-cited sources were reopened. The sources support the registered scope, task content, broad adoption signals, succession anchor, outsourcing indicators, M&A context, and four-digit output projection. Numeric primitives remain heavily transformed or estimated, but their population mismatches, uncertainty directions, and missing direct evidence are disclosed, ranges are wide, and the MODEL_CONDITIONED action is appropriate. No correction or honest evidence-state downgrade identified in this review would by itself change the base CONDITIONAL tier or VALIDATE_ASSUMPTIONS action.
